That exercise is the simple, powerful,
and incredibly life-changing movement
known as the chair squat.
Yes, you heard that right. Standing up
and sitting down from a chair done the
right way is one of the most powerful

It may look simple, but it activates the
largest muscle groups in your body,

These are the same muscles you need for

Every time you stand up from a chair,

You control the depth, you control the

Here's how you do it safely.
Sit on the edge of a sturdy chair with
your feet flat on the floor about
shoulder width apart.
Your chest stays tall, your shoulders
relaxed, and your eyes looking forward.
Gently lean forward just slightly from
your hips. Press through your heels and
slowly stand up. Do not rush.
At the top, stand tall without locking
your knees, then slowly bend your hips
and knees and lower yourself back down
under control until you gently touch the
chair.
Again, that is one repetition.
If needed, you can place your hands

you can cross your arms over your chest.
Now, let me tell you something powerful.
Studies show that seniors who can

This single movement directly reflects

When you perform this movement

Walking produces some of these benefits,

One fall can change your life forever.

Every time you stand up and sit down

The answer is simple and flexible.
